A mob cap or mob-cap is a round, gathered, or pleated cloth (usually linen) [[hat]] consisting of a caul to cover the hair, a frilled or ruffled brim, and (often) a [[hat ribbon|ribbon band]] to keep it in place (modern versions use elastic). 

Worn as an indoors hat by married women in the 18th & early 19th century. Depending on the location and era, it was also worn by [[maid]]s as an alternative to the stereotypical maid headdress. During the time period, it was called a [[bonnet]] but is n

A picture hat is an elaborate woman's [[hat]] with a wide brim, worn with [[gown]] or in [[formal]] settings. They resemble [[sun hat]] and are often heavily decorated with [[hat flower]], [[hat feather]] or other [[hat ornament]]. This style of hat was featured in virtually every decade of the 20th century and has a history dating back to at least the 18th century, as shown in pictures by 18th-century English painters such as Reynolds and Gainsborough.

A [[helmet]] worn by [[American football]] players. Early designs were made of leather, while modern ones are made of impact-resistant polymers. It generally has a face mask made of metal bars, and sometimes a visor.

h1. See also
* [[American football uniform]]
* [[Tag Group:Headwear]]

Helmet worn batters and other offensive players of a [[baseball]] team to protect them from the hard impact of a baseball.

h4. See Also
* [[baseball cap]], the one worn by the defensive players such as the pitcher.
* [[baseball uniform]]

A steel helmet as developed and used by the British army, also known as battle bowler, Tommy helmet or doughboy helmet. Similar in design to medieval kettle helmets, it has a domed top and wide brim. It was used by British and Commonwealth forces from World War 1 until the end of World War 2.

A type of helmet meant to be used while [[diving]] alongside some kind of [[diving suit]]. 

For the copper [[diving helmet]] found with older diving suits, see [[standard diving dress]].
A [[helmet]] that is reminiscent to a [[fishbowl]] or [[bubble]].  A common design used for [[space helmet]]s and [[diving helmet]]s in science fiction.

A yellow helmet worn around construction sites commonly made of heavy reinforced plastic. Japanese helmets are commonly depicted with a green stripe and a +.

Commonly worn by [[Met (mega man)|]]s in the [[Mega Man (series)|]] series and [[Engineer (tf2)|]] of [[Team Fortress 2]].

A type of small [[flashlight]] (or other similar illuminating device) worn on the head to provide light while leaving the hands free. 

Often attached to a [[headband]] or [[hard hat]] (such as a [[mining helmet]]), but may also be affixed directly to the head.

A medieval-style [[helmet]] that completely encloses the head. It offers absolute protection at the expense of being cumbersome and limiting vision greatly. This is the classic knightly armor and is generally build around the entire suit of armor. This is separate from a helmet with a built-in face mask since that is the addition of a component to the helmet while the helm is one-piece.

For depictions of the steering mechanism of a ship, use the [[ship's_wheel]] tag.

h4. See Also

* [[Ta

A type of helmet that is designed with [[horns]] on them.

Use [[horned headwear]] for other headwear.

h4. See also
* [[Horns]]

Ambiguous tag, do not use.

For the Pokémon, see [[kabuto_(pokemon)]]. For the Japanese helmet, see [[kabuto_(helmet)]].

A [[combat helmet]] that was used by the U.S. [[military]] since World War II, Korean War, Vietnam War until 1985. Well-known for its characteristic dome shape.

h4. See Also
* [[PASGT helmet]]
* [[Tag Group:Headwear]]

h1. External links
* "Wikipedia: M1 Helmet":https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M1_helmet

A [[helmet]] used to protect the user's head while riding a [[motorcycle]]. Although it covers the entire [[head]] like a [[helm]], it should not be tagged as such because it is not designed to protect the face.

h4. See also

* [[bikesuit]]
* [[biker clothes]]

A spiked [[helmet]] of Prussian origin. It is typically associated with the Prussian and Imperial German army.

It is still worn currently in Portugal, Sweden, and many South American countries.

A [b]flight helmet[/b] is a special type of [[helmet]] primarily worn by [[pilot]]s or (military) [[aircraft|aircrew]]. It largely replaced the softer [[aviator cap|aviator hats]] in modern times. Flight helmets provide impact protection. They often feature a visor, [[headset]] or [[microphone]] for both communication and noise attenuation, mounting system for [[night vision device]] and [[head-mounted display]], as well as compatibility with [[oxygen mask]]s.

h1. See also
* [[flight goggles

A type of [[helmet]] worn in 15th Century Europe, evolving from the earlier [[bassinet]] and into the later [[armet]] and close helmet.

Sallets are characterized by "tails" of varying length to protect the back of the neck. They may or may not have visors, which can be hinged or a solid part of the helmet.

A rounded see-through [[helmet]] designed for [[astronaut]]s.

h4. See also

* [[Fishbowl helmet]]
* [[Spacesuit]]

The [i]Stahlhelm[/i] (lit. "steel [[helmet]]") was a [[combat helmet]] used by Germany during [[World War I]] and [[World War II]]. It replaced the [[Pickelhaube]] used during [[World War I]].

Other countries have used Stahlhelme at one time or another, including the Argentine Army, the Chinese Nationalist Army, Hungary, Ireland until 1940, Spain, Switzerland, and the Yugoslav People's Army until 1959. The helmets are also used by the Bolivian Army, the Chilean Army (in ceremonial services),

A protective piece of equipment issued to [[tank]] or [[military vehicle|vehicle]] crew, primarily designed to withstand concussions against the [[vehicle interior]]. They come in different designs and shapes depending on what nation and time period. One of the most recognizable tank helmets is the Soviet/Russian TSh-4.

h4. See also
* [[combat helmet]] [tn](Worn by frontline combatants in battle.)[/tn]
* [[Tag Group:Headwear]]

A helmet that is adorned with wings on both sides. It's seen in ancient depictions of the greek god Mercury. however in the 19th century, the winged helmet became widely used to depict Vikings and Norse gods.

h4. See also

[[winged hair ornament]]

Also known as hirabitai. A three-pointed tiara-like ornament worn by the empress [[hina ningyou]].

For the object called 釵子 in the Heian period, use [[kanzashi]].

h4.See also

* [[maetenkan]]
* [[fengguan]]

A [[hat]] made of leather or similar material, worn by aviation [[pilot]]s and motorsport drivers to keep their head warm and to provide it some protection. Often made with ear flaps. Superseded by [[helmet|helmets]] when [[cockpit|cockpits]] became enclosed.

h4. See also
* [[Flight goggles]]
* [[Ushanka]]

A [[Chinese clothes|Chinese]] black hat with two wing-like flaps; also called [i]toujin[/i], [i]putou[/i].

The Korean [i]samo[/i] is similar in design - though a white version is worn during mourning. Also, men who'd never been married would wear the hat with two wings, and if they ever remarried, one of the wings were removed.

A traditional Middle Eastern square-shaped headdress which is generally worn on the head or around the neck like a scarf. Traditionally white with black or net-like red pattern.

There are many different terms to refer to it:

* keffiyeh or kufiya (Arabic: كوفية‎ kūfiyyah, meaning "from the city of Kufa" (الكوفة); plural كوفيات kūfiyyāt)
* ghutrah (غُترَة)
* shemagh (شماغ)
* ḥaṭṭah (حَطّة)
* mashadah (مَشَدة)
* chafiye (Persian: چَفیِه‎) 
* dastmal yazdi (Persian: دستمال یزدی‎)
* ceme

An object that isn't conventional headgear atop someone's head. If it the object cover's the wearers face, such as a bag, use an [i]over head[/i] tag, such as [[bag over head]] instead of [[bag on head]].

Use this tag for [b]objects that seem spontaneously placed on the character's head[/b], rather than fashioned into a head accessory (such as commonly found in  [[necktie on head]], where the necktie is fashioned into a headband). 

- Another example, if there is fruit on a character's hat,

When a bowl is used as a hat. Generally seen on [[Sukuna Shinmyoumaru]]. 

Not to be confused with [[bowler hat]].

A character has a [[book]] or [[book stack]] on their head. In Western nations, walking with a book on one's head is a traditional way to practice poise and posture.

A [[Japanese clothes|Japanese]] [[hood]] worn in [[winter]]. Not to be confused with a [[hijab]].

A cap to protect the wearer’s hair from getting wet while taking a shower or bath. Usually covers the top of the head.

Example: post #188107

Not to be confused with a [[shampoo hat]].

A flat-woven Japanese cotton fabric. Traditionally used as a multipurpose cloth, whereas in contemporary Japan terrycloth has become more commonplace for any other practical use. It is still used for decorative purposes or for apparel such as [[headscarf|head scarves]].
The turban is a type of headwear based on cloth winding. Featuring many variations, it is worn as customary headwear primarily by Sikh men and women.

A type of capless [[hat]], consisting of an eyeshade and something to fasten it to the head, such as a rim or an elastic strap.

Not to be confused with [[peaked cap]], which is associated with [[police uniform|police]] or [[military uniform]].

Headdress of the Huang people of China.

Similiar to qitou ('banner head') headwear of the Manchu people, but made from cloth, often with a fold-over design, and often features elements such as a [[hat tassel|tassel]] and dangling strips of cloth ([[lappet]]s).

Has varying different shapes - may be arched, triangular or horned-shape, for example.

An image that is framed at a tilted angle. Often used to fit tall characters into a wide image. The technique may also be used to make an image appear more dynamic or visually interesting.

The term originates in filmmaking, and is also known as the German angle or Batman angle.

Basically if you find yourself tilting your head to one side in order to see the picture in proper perspective, this tag is appropriate.

* !post #4243732: Dutch angle
* !post #4243734: No dutch a

A technique to show distance, typically employed in large outdoor areas. It results in a reduction in contrast and clarity due to the atmosphere scattering more and more light as the distance between the object and the viewer increases. [[Haze]] can increase the intensity of the effect.

"Wikipedia article":[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aerial_perspective]

A type of camera lens that takes photos in a distinct, distorted appearance, wherein straight lines appear increasingly curved the farther they are from the center of the image.  Use this tag for pictures that simulate this effect.

For the [[Bishoujo Senshi Sailor Moon]] character, see [[fish eye (sailor moon)]].

* !post #7603564

A wide-angle view of an area, broader than can be seen in one glance by the human eye. Typically produces a distorted image wherein some or all straight lines appear partially or completely curved, especially along the longer axis of the image.
External tools can be used to view these images properly.

If an image contains the full 360° view of an area, then also add the [[360_panorama]] tag.

Use this tag when the [[perspective]] of a picture appears to draw the viewer's eye towards a central point.  An example might be a city street as seen from the center of the road.

Convergence of the background of an image towards a single point is the key.  Do not confuse this for any use of [[perspective]] -- e.g., most [[checkered floor|checkered floors]]. For example, post #140887 just has [[perspective]], but post #1341879 has a [[vanishing point]].

It is possible for an image to cont

Pictures of the same character repeatedly in the same image that have been either copied and pasted or redrawn. Only a single outfit should be present, and most of its appearance should be preserved between variations, with only minor alterations to hair/clothing style, color, and accessories. If the difference is more significant, use [[multiple views]] instead.

This should be differentiated from sequential actions, such as a frame-by-frame rendition of a dance, or the stages of creating an

An instance of an object in a previous position. Usually depicted as a partial, off-set copy of the object, with varying degrees of differences.

A camera frame. A set of lines in the corners of an image, used by a camera to frame a person being photographed.

In fanart, camera frames are usually used to indicate the person is being photographed or video recorded. If there is a red ' ●REC ' or similar icon in the corner, add the [[recording]] tag too.

Camera frames are usually used by older [[camera]]s and [[camcorder]]s, that had a separate physical viewfinder for the eye. They're less common in modern smartphones.

h4. See also

Artwork that contains organized information for easy reading, identification, and compare/contrast. On Danbooru this usually takes the form of a tabular set of panels (e.g. [[expression chart]]), or a line of subjects to be compared visually (e.g. [[height chart]])

Not equivalent to a [[diagram]], which points out or explains the details of something instead of comparing multiple things. For the purposes of Danbooru for numeric data visualized in a spatial manner (e.g. a line chart, scatter p

A [[lineup]] of characters, usually in [[profile]], to compare their [[breasts|breast]] sizes.

The typical format of bust charts originated in 2004 with post #13199, a [[reference sheet]] for [[My-HiME]] published in [[Megami Magazine]].

Also referred to as 'chichikurabe' (lit. "breast comparison"). May or may not include actual numbered measurements.

An image depicting multiple members of a work's cast, usually in an informative style such as a cast list or hierarchy chart. Often the equivalent of multiple [[character profile|character profiles]] on a single page.

An image depicting the details of a design. A reference sheet usually consists of [[multiple views]] of the same subject from different angles, with notes on the design, and sometimes with details such as clothing or accessories drawn separately for characters. The image often has the character with certain [[clothes on/clothes off]].

For characters depicted in the same pose but in different clothing (as in post #1580637), use [[variations]].

This tag is for reference sheets of any subject

Visual artifacts reminiscent of the long gone analog days.

Blocky displacements and bent images are characteristic of this style. 

Please note that this tag is intended for intentional glitches. [[jpeg artifacts]] and [[scan artifacts]] are unintentional defects.

Also note that simply the appearance of digital squares may be an indication of [[digital dissolve]] rather than an actual glitch.

Sometimes used creatively as a means of censorship (see [[glitch censor]]).

A method of representing a 3D object on a 2D plane used in technical and engineering drawings. Unlike in [[perspective]] drawings or real life, parallel lines do not converge to a [[vanishing point]] and objects appear to be the same size regardless of distance.

Trim marks, aka crop marks, are used to show where to physically crop the image after printing it.  This is used when the size of the final product doesn't quite match a standard paper size.

Additionally, the extra margin allows the artist to "bleed" color beyond it and ensure that mistakes in trimming do not ruin the final product.

The Japanese word is Tonbo (トンボ).  Unfortunately, this is also a homophone for [[dragonfly]] in Japanese, which is what most "Pixiv searches":http://www.pixiv.

An additional layer that magnifies part of the illustration. It's a common technique used for backgrounds in character art. 

There are sometimes small, primarily 2D variations, such as:

* The layer being reversed.
* The layer being rotated.
* The layer being partially transparent, in greyscale, or having other changes in color.
* A character having a different expression.

If the layer is from a different 3D perspective, or there are larger 3D differences such as costumes, poses, or c

When there is a projection of someone somewhere in the background of an image.

If the layer is merely a magnification of another part of the image, possibly with 2D (but not 3D) variations, it's a [[zoom layer]] instead.

An animated image in the APNG format, an unofficial, backwards-compatible extension to the PNG format created by the group behind Mozilla/Firefox. Comparable to [[animated gif]] files, but with support for 24-bit colour and 8-bit transparency.

Oekaki (お絵描き) is Japanese for "scribble" or "doodle". On the Internet, an Oekaki is a message board system that revolves around computer art created by a local art program. Oekaki artists often post their art to other websites, such as personal pages or free gallery websites, in addition to the original board.

A photographic effect where circular [[depth of field|out-of-focus]] points of light appear in the background of the image.

Bokeh can represent known light sources in the background of an image, but can also occur in seemingly random places all over the image. The latter, known as "orbs":http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orb_%28optics%29, are from light reflected off of particles in the air which would otherwise not be visible.

[[Lens flare]] artifacts can look similar to bokeh, but are due to

Patterns of [[light]] projected onto a surface by light rays reflected off or refracted through another object. These can be seen on underwater surfaces, when light passes through materials like [[water]], glass, and the like. The bright ripples you can see on the bottom of a [[pool]] or on things close to it on a sunny day are this.

A type of optical distortion where the edges of an image have a colored, blurry halo or fringe. The RGB color channels may be noticeably offset from one another. In this respect it may sometimes resemble [[anaglyph]].

Diffraction spikes manifest as multiple spikes radiating from a point of light, similar to a [[lens flare]] which manifests as overall haziness and artifacts in the image.

[[Sparkle]], [[glint]], and [b]diffraction spikes[/b] all refer to similar patterns of bright lines radiating from a point. They are not clearly differentiated, and usage tends to be context-sensitive. In general:

An effect where part of the image appears [[blurry]] and out-of-focus. This could be the [[blurry foreground|foreground]], the [[blurry background|background]], or both.

This mimics the photographic effect that's caused by the camera being able to focus at only one distance at a time, making objects at other distances appear blurry. This is often used artistically to draw attention to one part of the image.

Foreshortening is the effect that an object is pointing at the viewer, by making it "shorter" than it really is (and usually enlarging the object). Getting this right is one of the "holy grails" of artists. Technically, all drawings that include the illusion of depth have foreshortening, so only use this tag if the foreshortening is a strong element of the image. Not to be confused with [[depth of field]], which refers to blurry, out-of-focus portions of an image. An image can conta

On Danbooru, this refers to a color gradient, which is a smooth, gradual shift from one color to another across an image, based on the position of a pixel relative to a defined point or line.

An image may still be considered [[monochrome]] if it just applies a gradient across what would otherwise be a black & white image, even if the gradient has a multitude of colors, e.g. post #61761 or post #141581.

A shading technique that uses intersecting lines of varying densities to simulate varying levels of darkness. A lightly colored object will have few lines, whereas a very dark object may have so many lines as to be solid black. Crosshatching is a variant of simple [[hatching_(texture)|hatching]] and may have two or more directions in which the lines lie.
Woven hatching is a shading technique where the artist 'hatch' or 'woven' to add shade to the art piece.

Anaglyph images are used to provide a stereoscopic 3D effect, when viewed with 2 color glasses (each lens a chromatically opposite color, usually red and cyan). Images are made up of two color layers, superimposed, but offset with respect to each other to produce a depth effect. Usually the main subject is in the center, while the foreground and background are shifted laterally in opposite directions. The picture contains two differently filtered colored images, one for each eye. When viewed thr

JPEG images can include an embedded thumbnail image, along with the camera/software metadata.

Sometimes the thumbnail doesn't match the full-size picture (because it wasn't updated after editing, or because of [i]shenanigans[/i]).

Old browsers such as [[Internet Explorer]] 8 and earlier had a unique quirk on how they rendered images when they're highlighted. Artists at the time took advantage of that and added "subliminal" images in their art as a bonus for those that use such browsers.

h6. Explanation of how it works

The trick works because those legacy versions of Internet Explorer highlight images using a non-transparent grid pattern, which cancels out one of the two images that have been interleaved into one ima
For images that have an embedded music/video track.

A stereogram is an optical illusion of depth created from flat, two-dimensional image or images. Originally, stereogram referred to a pair of stereo images which could be viewed using stereoscope or by crossing one's eyes and focussing on the resultant phantom image that appears between the two real ones. Other types of stereograms include [[anaglyph]]s and autostereograms.

Not to be confused with [[emphasis lines]], which do not indicate motion, but rather are used dramatically to draw attention to a specific part of the image.

An image defect that results in jagged or pixelated looking lines.

Aliasing artifacts are mostly caused by the artist resizing the image poorly, or by poor filtering during [[3D]] rendering.

When tagging, judge the image in full resolution, as the browser itself may produce aliasing artifacts when resizing the image. Especially consider the regularity of the undesired pattern and intended art style, as not all [[jaggy lines]] are due to a technical flaw.

This tag is for aliasing artifac

The [[Animated GIF]] image format is made to display a maximum of 256 colors in one image.  When an image containing more than 256 colors is saved as a GIF, the color limitation of the GIF format creates artifacts (defects) such as [[dithering]] (e.g. post #720057) or [[color banding]] (e.g. the background of post #76332).

Note that despite artifacting in high-color images, GIFs have legitimate uses, such as for [[pixel art]], low-color [[Oekaki]] images, and animated images.

h4. See also

A kind of compression artifact.

Prevalent in images uploaded from [[Twitter]] and [[Facebook]].

These can be either blockiness or mosquito noise and are usually seen in areas with high contrast, such as near lines. If in doubt, refer to some of the images with this tag. 

This tag should be used for images where these artifacts are noticeable when viewed normally, without zooming in or enhancing the contrast.

Uploading images with visible artifacts should be avoided.

A specific type of [[artistic error]] where the [[reflection]] in a [[mirror]] (or other reflective surface) doesn't match up with what it should be.

Having more than five fingers or toes. Usually an [[artistic error]] which amateur and professional artists alike are prone to on occasion. When parts of the image (typically graphic or sensitive contents such as sexual organs, gore, or offensive symbolism) were obscured, typically to comply with censorship laws.

On Danbooru, this typically encompasses various Japanese pornographic materials that were censored as a result of the Japanese law. An image that has not been [[censored]] by the artist. See [[decensored]] for images that have been decensored by a [[third-party edit]].

This tag applies only to genitalia, sex acts, body fluids, or other things that would normally be censored. It does not apply to [[nipples]], or to body parts that aren't fully visible. Note that this tag is mutually exclusive from [[censored]], and that both tags can be applied if one of the chracter's genitalia is left uncensored.

In Japan, it is illeg

The 'decensored' tag implies the original image was [[censored]] in its original version, but was decensored (drawn over the censorship) by a (for lack of a better word) decensorer. This is usually the case with [[third-party edit]]s of [[game cg]]s that originally contained [[mosaic censoring]].

All decensored posts should have the original as its parent. In addition, 'decensored' is different from both [[nude filter]] and [[uncensored]].

A post that is the same or inferior to an already existing post. This tag only applies to inferior posts that were uploaded [b]after[/b] an already existing identical post.

Duplicates should not be confused for the following:

* [[image sample]], an image typically used by sites to load images faster.
* [[md5 mismatch]], an post that has a different md5 from the file at the source.
* [[revision]], a post that has been revised by the artist from a previous version.
* [[variant set]], post

A post that is completely identical to its parent, and was either uploaded afterwards or has no source while their parent does, and thus is inferior for Danbooru purposes.

Pixiv and Twitter posts are often identical, even if they have different filesizes. The opposite of [[lossy-lossless]]: an image originally in a lossless format, such as PNG, that has been converted to a lossy format, such as JPEG.

It is Danbooru policy to delete [[third-party edit|third party]] lossless-lossy images when the original is available. 

h4. See also

* [[lossy-lossless]]
* [[image sample]]

An image that has been converted from a lossy image format to a lossless image format. Usually this is a PNG image ({{filetype:png}}) full of [[JPEG artifacts]], because it was originally a JPEG.

Lossy-lossless images should not be uploaded to Danbooru when the original is available. It is Danbooru policy to delete [[third-party edit|third-party]] lossy-lossless images when the original is available. See [[third-party edit]].

What you get when a country tries to use any language that's not their native language, and makes mistakes in the process. Given a link to the image, Danbooru will automatically find the best-quality version of the image available.

The progenitor of the modern [[chemistry|chemist]]. Alchemists are practitioners of alchemy, or the study of an early, unscientific form of chemistry practiced in the Middle Ages with aims including turning base metals into gold and discovering the elixir of perpetual youth, a universal cure for disease, and a universal solvent.

The [[atelier (series)|Atelier]] series of video games is designed around this premise, with the main protagonists being alchemists.

A geometrical figure with volume counting with a pair of parallel bases.
The most evocative prism has triangular bases (e.g. Post #742744). It is popular because of its ability to create [[rainbow|rainbows]] out of ordinary (polichromatic) light.

h5. Clarification

Cubes [b]are[/b] prisms too, but those  are better off tagged as [[cube|cubes]].
Pyramids [b]are not[/b] prisms.

The bending of light as it passes through a semi-transparent or transparent medium like water or glass.  Refraction is what makes a straight stick look bent underwater, and what makes everything look fisheyed through curved glass.

Ripples of light caused by this effect underwater or at the bottom of a pool are called [[caustics]].

Due to their size and the amount of light they produce, chandeliers are usually installed as focal points in large rooms such as [[dining_room|dining rooms]], lounges, or [[stairs|staircases]].

A traditional Japanese cosmetic practice of shaving off the eyebrows and painting on new ones, often in a distinctive comma shape or in a trapezoidal shape.

Common for women from the Heian period through the end of the Edo period, with even men joining in during the late Heian. Nowadays only seen in historical dramas and period costumes.

In wrestling, a lariat is when an attacking wrestler runs towards an opponent, wraps his arm around their upper chest and neck and then forces them to the ground. This move is similar to a clothesline, the difference being that in a [[clotheslining|clothesline]] the wrestler's arm is kept straight to the side of the wrestler during the move, while in the lariat the wrestler strikes their opponent with his arm.

A "magatama":https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magatama is a curved, "comma-shaped" bead or jewel, usually worn on a necklace. Often made of jade, magatama were produced in prehistoric Japan, and are associated with ancient Japanese mythology. (Similar jewels were found in ancient Korea, where they are called "gogok":[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gogok].) A type of sitting position where the feet are placed on the opposing thighs. A variation of the [[Indian style]] of sitting, it is a common meditation position among Hindu, Jain, and Buddhist contemplative practitioners (such as those of [[yoga]]). Known in Japanese as [i]kekkafuza[/i] (結跏趺坐). 

Not to be confused with Lotus Blossom position(example: post #2840969). Use combinations of [[upright_straddle]], [[indian_style]] and [[leg_lock]] for latter.

h4. See Also
* [[half lotus position]

Wrapping one's arms around one's own legs, usually in a [[sitting]] position. Sometimes used to express melancholy or contemplation.

This tag also includes [i]taiikuzuwari[/i] ("sports sitting"), a manner of [[sitting]] practiced by Japanese students in gym classes.

See [[leg lock]] for wrapping one's legs around another person's body. See [[hugging another's leg]] for hugging another person's leg. See [[knees to chest]] or [[fetal position]] if the person is lying on their back or side.

A more relaxed form of [[sitting]] where the character is leaning back but not entirely [[lying]] down.

h4. See also

* [[Tag group:Posture]]

Formal Japanese [[sitting]] position. Formed by [[kneeling]] on the floor and then resting the buttocks on the heels, keeping the [[legs folded]] under the thighs and the top side of the feet flat on the ground.

If the legs are folded out to either side, it's instead known as [[wariza]], and if they are only to one side, it's called [[yokozuwari]].

[b]Wariza[/b] (割座), also known as "w-sitting" or "reverse tailor style sitting" in English, is a [[sitting]] posture whereby the butt is on the floor and the legs are bent backwards on each side of the body. It is the traditional informal sitting style for Japanese women. If the legs are folded out to only one side, then it is called [[yokozuwari]].

The other Japanese names are: 女の子座り ([i]onna-no-ko-zuwari[/i], girl's sitting), アヒル座り ([i]ahiru-zuwari[/i], duck sitting) or ぺたん座り ([i]petan-zuwa

A Japanese term for a feminine informal sitting posture, on the knees as in [[seiza]] but with the legs folded out to one side. If the legs are folded out to either side, it's [[wariza]].

The traditional male informal sitting posture is [[indian style|agura]].

* !post #3366949

* [[sitting]]
* [[seiza]]
* [[wariza]]

One or more characters are standing; their bodies are being supported by their feet, or by either foot.

Most often, standing characters are absolutely upright; search for {{standing bent_over}} or {{standing leaning_forward}} for when they are not.

This tag is still applicable where the character is in the middle of certain actions that keep upright posture such as [[walking]].

h4. See also

* [[Balancing]]
* [[Handstand]]
* [[Rearing]]
* [[Squatting]]
* [[Standing on one leg]]
*
Images depicting a character trying to maintain their, or an object's center of gravity.

Legs that are apart while in the [[standing]] position.

Not to be confused with [[spread legs]], where the person is [[lying]] down or [[bent over]].

Balancing on one leg with the other off the ground.

h4. See also

* [[contrapposto]] — Standing on two legs, but with most of the weight on one leg
* [[leg lift]]
* [[leg up]]

The act of moving forward while on [[all fours]].

The short loop animations that characters in sprite-based video games (such as fighting games) do when they're just standing there with no player input happening. 

Commonly depicted behaviors include breathing, fidgeting with weapons/objects, bobbing up and down, and adjusting clothes and/or hair.

h4. See also

* [[walk cycle]]

When a character is seen fully on the air, not touching the ground or any support.

The character should be in a setting which clearly shows that they're floating in the air compare to their surroundings, if not, simply use [[floating]], [[flying]] or [[jumping]] as applicable. Don't using this tag on background-less or otherwise ambiguous settings.

* [[flying]]
* [[jumping]]
* [[levitation]]

* [[floating]]
* [[flying]]
* [[tripping]]
* [[fallen down]]

Moving or hovering in the air, seemingly without the help of an outside propulsion system (i.e wings).

For the act of floating in water, use [[afloat]].

h4. See also

* [[flying]]
* [[airborne]]

The act of being in or moving through the air with the help of a propulsion system (usually wings, a magic broom or a mechanical device) or being hurled to the air for some distance by either a person or hit by something. 

h4. See also

* [[floating]]
* [[jumping]]
* [[midair]]

The act of propelling oneself into the air from a surface, usually through the flexion and sudden muscular extension of the [[legs]].

h4.See also

* [[hopping]]
* [[pouncing]]

When a character is seen [[jumping]] a short distance forward.

h4.See also

The act of fervently [[jumping]] towards a desired object or character.

* [[glomp]]
* [[imminent pounce]]

To move swiftly on foot so that both feet leave the ground during each stride. Differs from [[standing on one leg]] in that the character is leaning forward a bit, has their legs stretched out in a stride, and any loose clothing and/or hair is blowing away from the person's front side. A good example of the running pose is post #19166.

* [[girly running]]

When a character is visibly walking. In non-[[animated]] pictures, this is usually indicated by one foot being placed in front of the other.

category_path: Other postures potentially involving the whole body
wiki:
A stance in which a swordfighter is preparing to unsheathe their blade as the first step of an upward slicing attack. The drawing hand may be on the hilt of the sword (post #136185) or waiting slightly apart from it (post #759893) while the other hand holds the saya at an angle suitable for the draw.

Battoujutsu's fast draw is a signature technique of [[Himura Kenshin]].

Also use [[sheathed cut]] when an actual cutting is taking place.

category_path: Other postures potentially involving the whole body

category_path: Other postures potentially involving the whole body

A variant of this bends only one leg, with the other leg straight in the direction the head is facing.

Do [b]NOT[/b] apply this tag simply because the post features [[Ancient Egyptian]] elements (ex. the character is wearing [[Ancient Egyptian clothes]]).

El Salvador, officially the Republic of El Salvador is a country in Central America. It is bordered on the northeast by Honduras, on the northwest by Guatemala, and on the south by the Pacific Ocean. El Salvador's capital and largest city is San Salvador. The country's population in 2023 was estimated to be 6.5 million.

* "Wikipedia: El Salvador":[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Salvador]

The landlocked Oriental Orthodox Christian Afroasiatic East African nation. It is the 2nd-most populous in Africa. It was the only African nation to successfully defend itself against European colonization. Ruled by an emperor until 1975.

Finland, officially the Republic of Finland, is a sovereign state in Europe. A peninsula with the Gulf of Finland to the south and the Gulf of Bothnia to the west, the country has land borders with Sweden to the northwest, Norway to the north, and Russia to the east. Estonia is south of the country across the Gulf of Finland. Finland is situated in the geographical region of Fennoscandia, which also includes Scandinavia.

The [b]United Arab Emirates[/b], is a country in Western Asia located at the eastern end of the Arabian Peninsula. Its most populous city, [[Dubai]], is a global city and international hub. It has become less reliant on oil and gas, and is economically focusing on tourism and business.
The largest of the seven city-states that made up the [[United Arab Emirates]]. Due to its promience, the name "Dubai" is often used to refer to the entire country as a whole.

[b]The United Kingdom[/b] (UK), officially the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, is a country located off the north-western coast of continental Europe. The country includes the island of Great Britain, the north-eastern part of the island of Ireland, and many smaller islands.

The United Kingdom is a unitary state governed under a constitutional monarchy and a parliamentary system, with its seat of government in the capital city of [[London]]. It is a country in its own ri

England is one of the four constituent countries of the [[United Kingdom]]. It shares land borders with [[Scotland]] to the north and [[Wales]] to the west; the Irish Sea is to the northwest, the Celtic Sea to the southwest, while the North Sea to the east and the English Channel to the south separate it from continental Europe. Most of England comprises the central and southern part of the island of Great Britain in the North Atlantic. The country also includes over 100 smaller islands such as

The capital city of [[England]] and the [[United Kingdom]], situated around the River Thames. Host city of the [[2012 Summer Olympics]].

[b]The Falkland Islands[/b] (/ˈfɔː(l)klənd, ˈfɒlk-/ FAW(L)K-lənd, FOLK-; Spanish: [b]Islas Malvinas[/b] [ˈislas malˈβinas]) is an archipelago in the South Atlantic Ocean on the Patagonian Shelf.

As a British overseas territory, the Falklands have internal self-governance, but the [[United Kingdom]] takes responsibility for their defence and foreign affairs. The capital and largest settlement is Stanley on East Falkland.

h4. See also
* [[Falklands War]]

A northeastern part of the isle of [[Ireland]] governed by the [[United Kingdom]].

Historically, the region was separated from the rest of Ireland due to religious and political issues following the Irish independence from the UK in 1922, with the Protestant, or "loyalist" choosing to remain within the UK instead of joining the Catholic-majority "republican" Ireland. Conflicts between both sides, known as "the Troubles", ensued. Usually involved both sides attacking each other with paramilita

Scotland is a country that is a part of the [[United Kingdom]]. Occupying the northern third of Great Britain, it shares a border with [[England]] to the south and is bounded by the North Sea to the east, the Atlantic Ocean to the north and west, and the North Channel and Irish Sea to the southwest. In addition to the mainland, Scotland constitutes over 790 islands including the Northern Isles and the Hebrides.

[[Plaid]] [[kilt|kilts]] is a traditional garment from the Scottish Highlands.

Wales is a country that is a part of the [[United Kingdom]] and the island of Great Britain. It is bordered by England to its east and the Atlantic Ocean and Irish Sea to its west.

* "External Link - Wikipedia:Wales":http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wales

A state in the Pacific region of the [[United states|United States]] with a total area of approximately 163,696 square miles and a population of over 39.3 million, making it the most populous and third largest state in the United States by area. Known for its diverse geography, vibrant cities, and cultural influence, California plays a significant role in various industries, including entertainment, technology, agriculture, and tourism.

The name "California" originates from the Spanish coloni

A major city located in Southern [[California]], [[United States]]. It is the largest city in California and the second-largest city in the United States, after [[new york city|New York City]]. Often referred to as L.A., Los Angeles is known for its vibrant culture, diverse population, entertainment industry, and iconic landmarks.

The city of Los Angeles encompasses a vast metropolitan area that stretches across approximately 503 square miles (1,302 square kilometers). It is situated in a coa

A major city located on the west coast of the [[United States]] in the state of [[California]]. It is known for its stunning natural beauty, iconic landmarks, diverse culture, and vibrant atmosphere. San Francisco is situated on a peninsula between the Pacific Ocean and the San Francisco Bay, with the [[Golden Gate Bridge]] being one of its most famous symbols.

The city's history dates back to its founding in 1776 by Spanish colonists. It played a significant role in the California Gold Rush

A city in sourthern [[Florida (location)|]] and one of the most popular tourist destinations in the state.

Miami is the inspiration behind the [[Grand Theft Auto]] series' Vice City, and is very closely linked to the [[vaporwave]] aesthetic, thanks to it's use as  setting for the popular 80s cop show [[Miami Vice]].

The 50th state of the [[United States]]. Comprised the namesake chain of islands in the middle of the Pacific ocean.

Originally a Polynesian kingdom, it enjoyed some recognition by the west before it was annexed by the US in 1898 following a government-backed coup in 1893. It was later incorporated as the 50th state in 1959.

Best known as a tropical refuge and a diverse demographics, from native Hawaiian to American settlers and a substantial amount of population of Japanese descent.

The most populous city of the state of [[Nevada]], [[United States]]. Famous for its massive [[casino]] industry.

A city of southern New York State on New York Bay at the mouth of the Hudson River and capital of the State of New York. Founded by the Dutch as New Amsterdam, it was renamed by the English in honor of the Duke of York. It is the largest city in the [[United States]] and a major commercial and cultural center. Originally consisting only of Manhattan Island, it was rechartered in 1898 to include the five present-day boroughs of Manhattan, the Bronx, Brooklyn, Queens, and Staten Island.

Texas is a state in the [[United states|United States]]. Its abbreviation is TX. It is located on the southern mainland of the United States and shares a border with [[Mexico]]. The capital city is Austin and its largest city is [[Houston]]. Well known for its petroleum and agriculture industries, as well as its "wild west" stereotypes.

Not to be confused with [[Texas (Arknights)|]], the [[Arknights]] character of the same name.
The largest city of the American state of [[Texas]] near the Gulf of Mexico. It is a major shipping port and center for petrochemical industries.
[i]Utah is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the Western [[United States]].[/i] - "Wikipedia":[https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Utah&oldid=1212711089]

A country in South America. It shares borders with [[Argentina]] to its west and southwest and [[Brazil]] to its north and northeast. The Uruguay national football team has won the FIFA World Cup on two occasions. Uruguay won the inaugural tournament on home soil in 1930 (First World Cup) and again in 1950.

A country on the northern coast of South America. It has the world's largest known oil reserves and has been one of the world's leading exporters of oil, but oil quickly came to dominate exports and government revenues. The excesses and poor policies of the incumbent government led to the collapse of Venezuela's entire economy.

The [b]Byzantine Empire[/b] (Koine Greek: [i]Βασιλεία Ῥωμαίων[/i]), also known as the [b]Eastern Roman Empire[/b], was the continuation of the [[Roman Empire]] in its eastern provinces. 

It lasted from the 5th century AD until its fall to the [[Ottoman Empire]] in 1453. It was a powerful economic, cultural, and military force in the Mediterranean, with its capital in [[Constantinople]] (modern-day "Istanbul":https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Istanbul, Turkey). A country in Central Europe, consisting of the [[Czech Republic]] and [[Slovakia]], which existed from roughly 1918 to 1992 (with a gap during World War II). Existed in two periods: the interwar period, when it also controlled a part of modern-day Ukraine, and the Cold War, when it was under [[Soviet]] influence as the Czechoslovak Socialist Republic.

[b]The Holy Roman Empire[/b], also known as [b]The First Reich[/b] or [b]The Holy Roman Empire of the [[germany|German]] Nation[/b] after 1512, was a polity in Central and Western Europe, usually headed by the Holy Roman emperor. It developed in the [[medieval|Early Middle Ages]] and lasted for almost one thousand years until its dissolution in 1806 during the Napoleonic Wars.

On 25 December 800, Pope Leo III crowned Frankish king [[Charlemagne]] as Roman emperor, reviving the title in Wester

The [b]Ottoman Empire[/b] was the political entity that governed modern-day [[Turkey (country)|]] (and several Middle Eastern and Balkan areas) from 1299 to 1922.

[b]Prussia[/b] was a [[Germany|German]] state, which existed from 1525 until 1867, with the establishment of the North German Confederation, and, later, the [[German Empire]]. It was — and still is — renowned for its military prowess under leaders like [[Frederick the Great]] and [[Otto von Bismarck]].

[b]Rhodesia[/b] was a [[United Kingdom|British]] colony and later an unrecognised country in Africa, which is now [[Zimbabwe]] and parts of [[Zambia]]. Ruled by the white minority with similar apatheid-like segregation laws with [[South Africa]], the country collapsed in 1980 after the Rhodesian Bush war and replaced by Zimbabwe.

The Rhodesian military is known for their usage of [[short shorts]], and for creating the [[Rhodesian Brushstroke]] camouflage pattern.

The [b]Roman Empire[/b] (Latin: [i]Imperium Rōmānum[/i]) was the post-Republican period of ancient Rome. 

As a polity, it included large territorial holdings around the Mediterranean Sea in Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, ruled by emperors. 

From the accession of Caesar Augustus as the first Roman emperor to the military anarchy of the 3rd century, it was a principate with Italy as the metropole of its provinces and the city of Rome as its sole capital.

The Angel of Independence, most commonly known by the shortened name El Ángel de la independencia and officially known as Monumento a la Independencia ("Monument to Independence"), is a victory column on a roundabout on the major thoroughfare of Paseo de la Reforma in downtown [[Mexico City]].

[[Baseball]]-only [[stadium]] located in Denver, Colorado. Named after the brewery company and home to the Colorado Rockies [[major league baseball]] team.

It is currently home to the [[Los Angeles Lakers]] of the [[National Basketball Association]] and the [[Los Angeles Kings]] of the [[National Hockey League]].

Fenway Park is a [[baseball stadium]] located in [[Boston]], Massachusetts. Since 1912, it has been the home of the [[Boston Red Sox]], the city's American League baseball team.

The Golden Gate Bridge, connecting the San Francisco Peninsula to Marin County, is one of the longest and tallest suspension bridges. The Golden Gate Bridge is located in the United States.

A temporary [[racetrack]] is built around the stadium grounds for the [[Formula One]] Miami Grand Prix, integrating parts of the parking lot along with dedicated racing pavement. The circuit is known as the Miami International Autodrome, and is constructed and then disassembled each year, being used solely for the F1 race and it's supporting events.

A mountain in the Black Hills near Keystone, South Dakota, in the [[United States]]. It is most well known as an American national memorial for its sculpture called the Shrine of Democracy carved into its granite face. Sculpted by Danish-American Gutzon Borglum and his son, Lincoln Borglum, it features 60-foot (18 m) sculptures of the heads of four US Presidents: [[George Washington]] (1732–1799), [[Thomas Jefferson]] (1743–1826), [[Theodore Roosevelt]] (1858–1919), and [[Abraham Lincoln]

Niagara Falls is a group of three waterfalls at the southern end of Niagara Gorge that spans the border between the province of Ontario in [[Canada]] and the state of New York in the [[United states|United States]].  It is composed of the three waterfalls: Horseshoe Falls, American Falls, and Bridal Veil Falls.

The replacement for the [[World Trade Center]] that was destroyed by the terrorist attacks on [[9/11|September 11, 2001]]. Formerly called the Freedom Tower. 

The building was opened in 2014 and is the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere. It is located near where the original Twin Towers once stood.

A large complex of seven [[building]]s in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in [[New York City]] [[City]] that originally stood from 1973 to 2001. More often referred to as the [b]Twin Towers[/b], in reference to its two tallest buildings, a pair of landmark buildings that stood there. The center was targeted as part of a terrorist attack on [[9/11|September 11, 2001]], leading to the destruction of the Twin Towers.

Oracle Park is a [[Major League Baseball]] stadium in the SoMa neighborhood of [[San Francisco]], California. Since 2000, it has been the home of the [[San Francisco Giants]]. Previously named Pacific Bell Park, SBC Park, and AT&T Park, the stadium's current name was purchased by the Oracle Corporation in 2019.

A famous landmark in Seattle, Washington, built for the 1962 World's Fair. An elevator leads visitors to the viewing deck and restaurant.

Most pictures of it are taken from Queen Anne Hill facing south toward downtown. In real life, it is shorter than most of the downtown buildings and is placed quite a distance away from them.

The statue, a gift to the [[United states|United States]] from the people of [[France]], is of a robed female figure representing Libertas, the Roman goddess of freedom, who bears a torch and a tabula ansata (a tablet evoking the law) upon which is inscribed the date of the American Declaration o
The official residence and workplace of the president of the United States, located in Washington D.C.

It is surrounded by numerous opulent imperial gardens and temples including Zhongshan Park, the sacrificial Imperial Ancestral Temple, Beihai Park, and the Jingshan Park. The head shrine of the kami Inari, located in the Fushimi ward of [[Kyoto]]. Known for its long tunnel of [[multiple torii]].

Several walls were being built from as early as the 7th century BC by ancient Chinese states; they were later joined together by [[Qin Shi Huang]] , the first

Hiroshima Peace Memorial, commonly called the Atomic Bomb Dome or A-Bomb Dome (原爆ドーム Genbaku Dōmu), in [[Hiroshima]], Japan, is part of the Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park and was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1996. The ruin serves as a memorial to the people who were killed in the [[atomic bombings of hiroshima and nagasaki|atomic bombing of Hiroshima]] on August 6, 1945.

The Church of the Savior on Spilled Blood is a church located in Saint Petersberg, Russia. The outside architecture of the church features onion domes set at varying heights.

A Catholic [[cathedral|basilica]] in Cologne, North Rhine-Westphalia, [[Germany]], officially called the [b][i]Hohe Domkirche Sankt Petrus[/i] (Cathedral Church of Saint Peter)[/b]. It is Germany's most visited landmark.

At 515 feet (157 meters) tall, it was the tallest building in the world for the first 10 years after its completion from 1880 to 1890 and remains the the tallest twin-spired church in the world, the second tallest church in Europe after Ulm Minster, and the third tallest chur

Originally the [b]Flavian Amphitheatre[/b], an elliptical amphitheatre in the centre of the city of [[Rome (city)|]], [[Italy]], the largest ever built in the Roman Empire, built of concrete and stone. It is considered one of the greatest works of Roman architecture and Roman engineering and is also famous as a [[gladiator]]ial arena. A wrought iron [[tower]] built in [[Paris]] by Gustave Eiffel for the 1889 World's Fair. The bronze-painted tower has two observation decks and restaurants in addition to the top floor, including a champagne bar and a reconstruction of Eiffel's office.

The iconic clock tower of [[Westminster Palace]] in London, United Kingdom. Simply known as the Westminster Clock Tower before 2012 when it was renamed during the Diamond Jubilee of Queen [[Elizabeth II]]. It houses the world famous bell Big Ben (the name of which is sometimes incorrectly used for the tower).

[b]Hagia Sophia[/b] (lit. 'Holy Wisdom'; Ancient Greek: Ἁγία Σοφία (Hagía Sophía), Turkish: Ayasofya), is a [[mosque]] and major cultural and historical site in Istanbul, [[Turkey (country)|Turkey]]. It was erected three times by the Eastern Roman Empire. The present Hagia Sophia is the third, built in 537 AD. A famous bell tower in the Italian city Pisa that started to lean during construction due to an unstable foundation. The tower was stabilized and its tilt slightly decreased by remedial work from 1993 to 2001.

A tidal island and mainland commune in Normandy, [[France]]. A closely related form of armor is spaulders, which are made of smaller overlapping plates that cover just the shoulder and top of the forearm. Due to continuous misuse of the former "spaulders" tag, on Danbooru, spaulders are now tagged as pauldrons.

h6. Other elements of arm armor
* [[Rerebrace]], for the upper arms.

category_path: Attire for shoulders/Other attire, on shoulders
wiki:
A [[jacket]] draped over the shoulders, rather than worn with the arms in the sleeves.

While this may be done for a variety of reasons, the most common reasons are to cover up oneself, use as an improvised blanket to stay warm, or use as an improvised [[cloak]] or [[cape]].

Use [[single bare shoulder]] when only one shoulder is exposed.

Sleeved clothing articles that rest just below a character's shoulder(s), at around the upper arm level. This includes clothing that has slipped or been pulled off the shoulders.

If they are down further, consider the [[clothes down]] tag.

For strapped clothing that has been pulled off the shoulders, use [[strap slip]] instead.

category_path: Attire for shoulders/Clothing physically designed to leave the shoulders bare
wiki: A [[strapless]] garment consisting of a continuous band of fabric across the torso, with the neckline stopping at the character's chest. Length ranges from covering the entire torso to just covering the breasts, or part of them; the latter two cases can additionally qualify as a [[bandeau]] or [[crop top]]. Traditionally, the only support they provided was due to the stretchiness of the fabric. Tube tops do not have any attached sleeves, meaning that they leave the shoulders/arms and upper chest

category_path: Attire for shoulders/Clothing physically designed to leave the shoulders bare
wiki:
A [[shirt]] that doesn't have any straps, neck, or sleeves. The shoulders and upper chest area are left [[bare shoulders|bare]].

A strapless shirt is similar to a [[tube top]], but not exactly the same. A tube top is generally shorter and has a tighter fit, while a strapless shirt is usually longer and looser. A tube top usually wraps tight around the breasts and doesn't cover the [[midriff]], while a strapless shirt usually covers the stomach and doesn't conform as tightly to the breasts.

category_path: Attire for shoulders/Clothing physically designed to leave the shoulders bare

The shoulders and upper chest area should be [[bare shoulders|bare]], which also implies the lack of a strapped [[bra]].

Strapless dresses must [b]not[/b] cover the shoulders or arms in any way outside of separate articles of

category_path: Attire for shoulders/Clothing physically designed to leave the shoulders bare
wiki:
A [[bra]] without straps, relying on an extra-wide band for breast support. 

They are designed to be worn with [[strapless]] clothing that would otherwise expose the bra straps. They achieve their strength through a longer underwire that encompasses more of the breast, and cups with added padding, boning, and shaping panels. Some have rubberized or silicone beading inside the top edge of the cup to help keep the bra attached to the breast. Some convertible bras allow the straps to be removed,

When an upper-body garment (such as a [[shirt]] or a [[jacket]]) only has one sleeve. If the garment has two sleeves of different lengths, use [[uneven sleeves]]. Do not use this tag for singular gloves, bridal gauntlets, detached sleeves etc.

When a character wears his or her clothes with both or one of its sleeves pushed or pulled up. This can be either to cool themselves off, to prevent the sleeves from getting dirty or wet or getting in the way of what they are doing, or simply due to the characters' taste in fashion. They also do this if they are preparing to deliver a blow to someone in a comical way or if they are getting ready for something difficult.

Do not confuse with [[sleeves rolled up]], in which cases the sleeves are

When a character wears their clothes with both or one of its sleeves rolled up. This can be either to cool themselves off, to prevent the sleeves from getting dirty or wet, because of clothing regulations (for example [[uniform|uniforms]]), or because of their taste in fashion.

Includes sleeves visibly only folded up once. Not to be confused with [[sleeves pushed up]], in which case the sleeves are only pushed up the arm, instead of being folded up.

A fabric tube worn over the arm or leg that compresses that part of the body. This is used to provide muscle support, improve blood circulation, reduce pain, and provide warmth. They're sold in various sizes to cover different portions of the arm or leg, such as covering the elbow, forearm, entire arm, thigh, knee, shin, and entire leg. They're used in both medical treatment and sports.

[[bike_shorts|Compression shorts]] operate in a similar manner as compression sleeves.

Raglan sleeves are sleeves attached to a garment via a diagonal seam from the armpit to the neckline (rather than a simple circular seam at the edge of the shoulder). They are often made in a different color than the torso of the garment and are constructed from one piece of material. Raglan sleeves are commonly used in sportswear, as they are designed to give greater freedom of movement for the arm and shoulder.

Pyramids [b]are not[/b] prisms.

A [[musical note]] used to represent a note with no discernable pitch. It is typically represented by a note with a x where the note head would usually be, and is mostly used in percussion.

In musical notation, a grace note is distinguished from a regular note by print size. A grace note is indicated by printing a note that is much smaller than a regular note, sometimes with a slash through the note stem (if two or more grace notes, there might be a slash through the note stem of the first note but not the subsequent grace note).

You can see an example at the very upper right corner of the sheet music in post #2863696.

A [b]cross potent[/b] (☩) is a form of heraldic [[cross]] with crossbars at the four ends, resembling T-shape.
This also applies to the Jerusalem cross, which is surrounded by four [[Greek cross]]es.

Not to be confused with [[cross crosslet]], with the ends of each arm crossed resembling the Latin cross.

The [b]Iron Cross[/b] ([i]Eiserne Kreuz[/i]) is a [[germany|German]] and Prussian [[military]] symbol and decoration [[medal]]. Its design consists of a black [[cross patty|cross pattée]] with white outline. Its use continues within the [[Bundeswehr]] service (after 1956).

Several video games like [[world war ii|WWII]]-themed shooters, [i][[Kantai Collection]][/i] and [i][[Azur Lane]][/i] will use Iron Crosses [[euphemism|to substitute the]] [[swastika]] in order to comply with [[censored sym

The [b]Orthodox cross[/b] (☦), also known as the Russian Orthodox or Byzantine or Suppedaneum cross, is a variation of the [[christianity|Christian]] [[cross]]. It is a symbol of the [[Russia]]n Orthodox Church and a distinctive feature of the cultural landscape of Russia. 
The cross has three horizontal crossbeams (top one is shorter) and the lower one is slanted, giving it 8 distinct points.

A [b]sun [[cross]][/b], solar cross, or wheel cross is a symbol consisting of an equilateral cross inside a circle.

Not to be confused with [[Celtic cross]], of which the arms extends longer past the circle.

Nowadays, the symbol is usually associated with [[heavy metal]] and [[punk]] to representing how they are not bound by anything and rebelling against the norm.

The ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic character that read "life". Egyptian gods are often portrayed carrying it by its loop, or bearing one in each hand, arms crossed over their chest.

The Ars Goetia, otherwise known as the Lemegton or The Lesser Key of Solomon, is a 17th-century grimoire describing 72 demons that Solomon is said to have evoked and confined within brass seals. All of them are given ranks, alignments, abilities, characteristics, and specific summoning instructions.

Examples in modern media are the Great Demons [[Gaap (umineko)|]], [[Ronove (umineko)|]], [[Furfur]] and [[Zepar]] in [[Umineko no Naku Koro Ni]].

The ☮ symbol, commonly known as the peace symbol.

Originally designed for the British nuclear disarmament movement by Gerald Holtom in 1958, it took on a more generic association with peace in general after being made into badges that were sold in the millions on American campuses. 

For the hand sign with the index and middle fingers raised, see [[V]].

h4. See also

* [[Tag Group:Symbols]]

One of two symbols composed of a Zero (Circle) and One (vertical line) overlaid on top of each other.

Under the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) 60417 standard, Graphical symbols for use on equipment, 0 is the symbol for "off" and 1 is the symbol for "on." Combining these two symbols you get either the symbol for "on/off" (a circle with a vertical line inside of it) or "stand-by" (an incomplete circle with an opening at the top and a vertical line sticking out of the opening).

h4. ☢

A symbol used to warn people that the substance is [[radioactive]]. This symbol is usually seen on the container used to store or transport them. The only place in public where you can see this symbol is in the [[X-ray]] room in hospitals and in nuclear medicine centers.

The [b][i]Reichsadler[/i][/b] ("Imperial Eagle") is a German [[heraldry|heraldic]] emblem used since the Holy Roman Empire as a symbol of the [[germany|German]] State, adopted by the German Empire as the national emblem. Its use as the National Emblem continued under the Weimar Republic. The Weimar version was re-adopted by the West Germany and modern-day Federal German Republic as the [i]Bundesadler[/i]. 

Despite its medieval origin, the term [i]Reichsadler[/i] in common English understandin

[[Staff]] with one [[snake]] spiraling up it. Used by Asclepius, the Greek God of healing and medicine, and associated with medical-related professions. Often confused with the [[Caduceus]].

A symbol used to identify emergency medical services.  
It features a six-pointed star (🞺), most commonly depicted blue with a white border, and usually featuring a [[rod of asclepius]] in the center.
It is commonly seen on [[ambulance|ambulances]] and [[hospital|hospitals]], as well as on [[medic|medics]] uniforms in various settings.

A symbol (☪︎) consisting of a [[crescent]] accompanied with a [[star (symbol)|]] (often five-pointed). Commonly associated with the [[Ottoman Empire]] and [[Islam]], also appears in many flags and [[heraldry|coats of arms]].

Ancient religious symbol or [[mandala]] used in numerous cultures throughout history ([[India]], etc.). Takes the form of a [[cross]] with four legs bent at 90 degrees.

The clockwise, or right-handed, swastika (卐) was appropriated by the [[nazi|Nazi Party]] as its emblem. This variant was turned 45 degrees in a white circle on a red background which is known as Hakenkreuz (hooked cross). The symbol continues to be widely associated with Nazism to the present day. 

Another user of the clock

Eight diagrams used in Taoist cosmology to represent the fundamental principles of reality, seen as a range of eight interrelated concepts. Each consists of three lines, each line either "broken" or "unbroken," representing yin or yang, respectively. Due to their tripartite structure, they are often referred to as "trigrams" in English.

Most popularly used on [[Yakumo Yukari]]'s [[tabard]] on her [[Imperishable Night]] outfit, as well as [[Yagokoro Eirin]]'s dress.

The dharmachakra or wheel of dharma is a symbol used in the Dharmic religions of Buddhism, Hinduism, and Jainism. It has a widespread use in Buddhism. In Hinduism, the symbol is particularly used in places that underwent religious transformation. The symbol also finds its usage in modern India.

It is now often associated with [[Nazi]] ideology in modern times because of its use by different [[Waffen-SS]] units during [[WWII]]. 

The [b]wolfsangle[/b] is often confused with the more ubiquitous [[swastika]].

The [b]zodiac[/b] is the "band" of the night sky roughly corresponding to the solar system's ecliptic plane. Western astrologers divide it into twelve signs, named for the constellation that is nearest the position of the Sun for one-twelfth of the year.  (These periods do not quite line up with the calendar months, so that the sign of the zodiac changes around the twentieth or twenty-first of the month.)

A mix of the [[mars symbol|mars]] (♂) and [[venus symbol|venus]] (♀) symbols.

It can be used to refer to characters that have an [[androgynous]] appearance, characters that are intersex/hermaphrodite/[[futanari]], or characters with a non-binary gender identity.

It can be shown pointing in any direction, and the direction is sometimes used to distinguish androgyne and intersex.

Closely related to and commonly interchanged with with the [[male-female symbol]] (⚥). Distin

The former may merely imply a romantic connection between a man and a woman, while the latter more explicitly indicates sexual activity between the two.

Compare [[penetration gesture]].

Often used to represent [[futanari]], [[futa without pussy]], intersex, hermaphrodite or [[genderswap|genderswapped]] characters.

Closely related to and commonly interchanged with the [[androgyne symbol]], ⚨.

Not to be confused with the transgender symbol.

A pedestrian crossing sign is a [[road sign]] to alert oncoming traffic that they're either approaching a [[crosswalk]] or they're approaching an area where pedestrians frequently cross the street.

These signs at minimum usually depict a silhouette figure walking and may include the figure walking in a crosswalk.  These signs are similar to [[school crossing sign]]s, which serve a similar purpose but depict the figure of a boy and girl carrying a book in their hands.

[[Road sign]] that shows the permitted maximum speed (in kilometer or mile) of a vehicle. It comes in various formats. In many countries such as the EU, Russia and Japan, they are simply encircled numbers. While in the US, the sign takes a rectangular shape and often contain all caps inscription that accompany the number.

Not to be confused with [[no entry sign]], which in Japan is a red circle with a diagonal bar (post #3627577 is an example).

The [[flag]] of transgender pride. Various designs exist, but the most commonly used design features pink, sky blue, and white stripes, representing transfeminine, transmasculine, and non-binary people respectively.

h4. Examples

* !asset #20136646: Monica Helms's Transgender Pride flag, created in 1999

h4. See also

* [[Rainbow flag]]
* [[Bisexual flag]]
* [[Non-Binary flag]]
* [[Lesbian flag]]
* [[Pansexual flag]]
* [[Intersex flag]]
* [[Aromantic flag]]

h4
Clothing or an object that has printings resembling the [[Transgender flag]].

The ⚧ symbol.

It is a combination of the [[mars symbol]] (♂), the [[venus symbol]] (♀), and the [[androgyne symbol]] (⚨).

Not to be confused with the [[male-female symbol]] (⚥).

A form of distress where a person feels that their gender identity does not match their sex as assigned at birth.

h4. External Links
* "Gender dysphoria - NHS":https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/gender-dysphoria/

A post about a person transitioning to another gender. This may include direct depictions of it, or implied or stated gender transitioning (post #3934361). 

Don't use this tag if there's no indication of gender transitioning actually taking or having taken place.

h4. See also

* [[Mastectomy Scar]]
* [[Transgender Symbol]]
* [[Transgender Flag]]
* [[Androgynous]]
* [[Futa without pussy]]

The process of removing one's [[breasts]].

h4. See also
* [[mastectomy scar]]

A type of [[scar on chest|chest scar]].

These scars tend to come in symmetrically placed pairs, placed on lower chest, right below pectorals. They are sometimes slightly curved upwards.

They are associated with [[mastectomy]] (colloquially referred to as "top surgery" in LGBT circles) - a procedure that removes a person's breasts. Common for transgender men, for whom it is used to achieve a more masculine appearance, but it is also used for breast cancer or gynecomastia patients.

h4. Se

A noticeable stiffening of the [[clitoris]], similar to a [[erection]]. Length may vary. The phenomenon is known as a clitoral erection and is a real sexual response in humans, caused by increase blood flow to the clitoris. The clitoris is the homolog to the penis which means it shares many functions with it, such as erections.

h4. See also

* [[Huge clitoris]]

h4. External links

* "Wikipedia: Clitoral erection":[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clitoral_erection]
A [[clitoris]] that is prominently large, but not unrealistic.  May protrude prominently from the [[clitoral_hood]].

A [[clitoris]] that is unusually large. May resemble an erect penis. 

In humans testosterone can enlarge the clitoris. Some may purposefully make their clitoris larger as a body modification. Female [[bodybuilder]]s who use steroids may experience this unintentionally. [[Transgender]] men who take testosterone as hormone replacement may purposefully seek out this result. Some posts under [[cuntboy]] may depict transgender man, making the tag applicable in those cases.

h4. See also

* [[e

Using the [[clitoris]] to penetrate another in a sexual manner.

Any image centered around baking sweets, desserts and bread. An establishment entirely devoted to baking is a [[bakery]].

A hobby where yarn is thread together to create cloth by making little loops called stitches on a row, and then threading the yarn through these stitches on the next row while creating stitches for the next row, and so forth.
